evidence.snippet: Innoviz is a leading manufacturer of high-performance LiDAR sensors and Perception Software that bring vision to the automotive, drone, logistics, sidewalk delivery and other industries.

**Innoviz has developed two high-performance automotive-grade solid-state [LiDAR sensors](https://innoviz.tech/lidar-sensors), plus industry-leading [perception software](https://innoviz.tech/perception-software).**

items.notes: LiDAR sensors (InnovizOne, InnovizTwo family, InnovizThree, InnovizSMART) plus perception software. Explicitly sold for drone/aerial applications.

products_categories.notes: Aerial application (drones) listed alongside automotive, robotics, security on site nav. InnovizTwo explicitly mentioned for industrial drones in the LiDAR technology page.

items.product_type: solid-state LiDAR sensor, up to 1km range

items.name: Perception Software

evidence.snippet: **Innoviz has developed two high-performance automotive-grade solid-state [LiDAR sensors](https://innoviz.tech/lidar-sensors), plus industry-leading [perception software](https://innoviz.tech/perception-software).**

products.notes: Product pages for InnovizSMART, InnovizThree were skipped in crawl. Full specs may differ.

evidence.snippet: Innoviz is the first LiDAR provider to be selected by a major automaker (BMW) for the mass-production of Level 3-5 autonomous vehicles, as well as several partnerships with the world’s leading Tier 1 automotive suppliers, like HARMAN, HiRain, Magna and Aptiv. Innoviz is backed by top-tier strategic partners and investors.

Innoviz has partnered with Tier 1 suppliers to improve the production of its solid-state LiDAR sensors made of mostly semiconductor components (primarily chips), which lowers cost and increases performance and reliability.

readiness.notes: Mass production with BMW for Level 3-5 autonomous vehicles. ISO9001:2015 certified. Tier 1 supplier partnerships for scaled manufacturing.

tagline: Israeli solid-state LiDAR sensor manufacturer producing automotive-grade units (InnovizOne through InnovizThree) with ranges from 250 m to 1 km, plus complementary AI/ML perception software and SMART/security applications. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of US defense adoption or integration remains thin.
